source.definition: a large, strong mammal covered with thick fur

explanation.sourceText: The English word "bear" originates from Old English "bera", which is related to the Proto-Indo-European root for "brown", referring to the animal's fur color.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „bear” provine din engleza veche „bera”, care este legat de rădăcina proto-indo-europeană pentru „maro”, referindu-se la culoarea blănii animalului.

source.additional: Bears are large, powerful mammals found in various habitats.

target.definition: un mamifer mare și puternic cu blană deasă

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "urs" derives from Latin "ursus", which denotes a large, powerful bear.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „urs” derivă din latină, din „ursus”, care desemnează un animal mare și puternic.

target.additional: Der Bär ist ein wichtiges Symbol in Rumänien und erscheint häufig in der Folklore.

examples.sourceText: There are many animals in the zoo, including a bear.
examples.targetText: La grădina zoologică sunt multe animale, inclusiv un urs.

examples.sourceText: The bear is a large animal.
I saw a bear on television.
examples.targetText: Am văzut un urs la televizor.

learningTips.text: Remember the Romanian word "urs" sounds like the start of "urgent"—imagine a bear urgently searching for honey.
Think of the Latin root "ursus" to connect the English "bear" and Romanian "urs" as related big animals.
Picture a bear roaring 'urr!' to recall the Romanian "urs" pronunciation easily.
Link the word "bear" with famous stories like Goldilocks to remember the Romanian "urs" in a fun way.
